perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding clause 3.1 of this Agreement, the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information : a) to its and its Affiliate’s Representatives , provided always that the Receiving Party procures that such Representatives shall comply with the confidentiality obligations as set out in clause 3 of this Agreement , and the Receiving P arty agrees to be liable for the actions or omissions of such Representatives in relation to the Confidential Information as if they were the actions or omissions of the Receiving Party ; and b) as may be required by an order of any court of competent jurisdic tion or governmental body in which case t he Receiving Party shall, to the extent permitted by law, use reasonable endeavours to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of any such requirement prior to any disclosure so that the Disclosing P arty may seek a protection order or other appropriate remedy. If no protective order or remedy is obtained by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that it is legally required to disclose .
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding clause 3.1 of this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information : a) to its and its Affiliate’s Representatives , provided always that the Recipient procures that such Representatives shall comply with the confidentiality obligations as set out in clause 3 of this Agreement , and the Recipient agrees to be liable for the actions or omissions of such Representatives in relation to the Confidential Information as if they were the actions or omissions of the Recipient ; and b) as may be required by an order of any court of competent jurisdic tion or governmental body in which case t he Recipient shall, to the extent permitted by law, use reasonable endeavours to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of any such requirement prior to any disclosure so that the Disclosing P arty may seek a protection order or other appropriate remedy. If no protective order or remedy is obtained by the Disclosing Party, the Recipient shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that it is legally required to disclose .
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Receiving Party' with 'Recipient'. The in-text contradiction is that the rest of the section text keeps referencing the term 'Disclosing Party'. It contradicts the definition previously defined in the first page of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party : a Party to this Agreement which receives the Confidential Information from the other P arty;
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both refer to "Receiving Party". The changed text replaced "Receiving Party" with "Recipient" while the contradicted text defines "Receiving Party". This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text provides a definition for "Receiving Party" which is no longer used in the updated section, potentially leading to confusion about the parties involved in receiving confidential information. This is an example of Misaligned Terminology as it uses multiple terms interchangeably, and leads to conflicting obligations, thus it introduces a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party ’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Party) either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Party’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Organisation’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Organisation) either return to the Disclosing Organisation or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Organisation including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Organisation’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files .
perturbation.explanation: Replaced Disclosing Party with Disclosing Organisation. Since Disclosing Party is already defined, this new reference introduces ambiguity and contradiction, specially since the definition wasn't updated. There is an in-text contradiction with other sections using the term 'Disclosing Party', creating uncertainty and leading to potential legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding clause 3.1 of this Agreement, the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information : a) to its and its Affiliate’s Representatives , provided always that the Receiving Party procures that such Representatives shall comply with the confidentiality obligations as set out in clause 3 of this Agreement , and the Receiving P arty agrees to be liable for the actions or omissions of such Representatives in relation to the Confidential Information as if they were the actions or omissions of the Receiving Party ; and b) as may be required by an order of any court of competent jurisdic tion or governmental body in which case t he Receiving Party shall, to the extent permitted by law, use reasonable endeavours to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of any such requirement prior to any disclosure so that the Disclosing P arty may seek a protection order or other appropriate remedy. If no protective order or remedy is obtained by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that it is legally required to disclose .
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Disclosing Party' with 'Disclosing Organisation'. Since 'Disclosing Party' is used and likely defined elsewhere in the contract (as suggested by the contradiction text referring to 'Disclosing Party'), but 'Disclosing Organisation' is not, this creates a clear contradiction. The contradicted text relies on the term 'Disclosing Party', so the introduction of a new, undefined term creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, directly conflicting with the existing obligation in the contradicted text. This fits the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition as it introduces multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The contradicted text uses 'Disclosing Party' to define the obligations of the Receiving Party, so using 'Disclosing Organisation' would cause confusion.

perturbation.original_text: Neither of the Parties make any express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy , reliability or completeness of the Confidential Information, expressly disclaiming any and all liability that may be based on the Confidential Information, including any errors therein or omissions there from.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither of the Entities make any express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy , reliability or completeness of the Confidential Information, expressly disclaiming any and all liability that may be based on the Confidential Information, including any errors therein or omissions there from.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Parties' with 'Entities' in section 7.1. This causes confusion because 'Parties' is defined at the beginning of the document and consistently used. The in-text contradiction arises as it's unclear whether 'Entities' refers to the same defined 'Parties' without any definition for the new term, leading to uncertain interpretation of the legal agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement is int ended to or shall be deemed to establish any partnership or joint venture between the Parties, create any commercial agency relationsh ips between the Parties or authorise any Party to make or enter into any commitments for or on behalf of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Parties' to 'Entities' in section 7.1 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text defines 'Parties' at the beginning of the document, and the contradicted text refers specifically to 'Parties'. By introducing the undefined term 'Entities,' it becomes unclear whether the provisions regarding partnerships, joint ventures, agency relationships, and authorized commitments still apply, because they are exclusively defined in relation to 'Parties'. The introduction of 'Entities' where 'Parties' was previously used creates a direct contradiction and ambiguity, affecting the enforceability and interpretation of the agreement as it relates to the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party irrevocable agrees that the courts of England and Wales shall have exc lusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim (including non -contractual disputes or claims) arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Organisation irrevocably agrees that the courts of England and Wales shall have exc lusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim (including non -contractual disputes or claims) arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Party' with 'Organisation'. This causes confusion because 'Party' is defined at the beginning of the document and consistently used, specially within the same sentence for other references. The in-text contradiction arises as it's unclear whether 'Organisation' refers to the same defined 'Party' without any definition for the new term, leading to uncertain interpretation of the legal agreement. This could invalidate the jurisdiction clause as it's unclear who is agreeing to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party referred to as a “ Party ” and the parties jointly referred to as the “ Parties ”. BACKGROUND A. The Parties intend to enter into discussions relating to the Purpose which will involve the exchange of Confidential Information between them. B. The Parties have agreed to comply with this Agreement in connection with the disclosure and use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Party', which is defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Organisation', which is not defined. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text defines 'Party', and the changed text uses a new, undefined term, making it unclear who is agreeing to the jurisdiction clause. As described in the Perturbation Explanation, it's unclear whether 'Organisation' refers to the same defined 'Party' without any definition for the new term, leading to uncertain interpretation of the legal agreement. This is a strong contradiction because it undermines a fundamental aspect of the contract (jurisdiction) by introducing ambiguity about the parties involved. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
